tasks.title: Add Hive Mind domain models to pkg/models/
tasks.description:
Add new model types for the Hive Mind feature to the existing pkg/models/ package. This is a Go project using go module 'github.com/valter-silva-au/ai-dev-brain'. Create a NEW file pkg/models/hive.go (do NOT modify existing files in pkg/models/). The file must have package declaration 'package models' and define these types with yaml struct tags:

1. ProjectStatus (string type with constants: ProjectActive='active', ProjectArchived='archived', ProjectPaused='paused')
2. Project struct with fields: Name, RepoPath, Purpose, TechStack ([]string), Status (ProjectStatus), LastUpdated, Tags ([]string), DefaultAI, RelatedProjects ([]string), KnowledgeEntryCount (int), DecisionCount (int), ActiveTaskCount (int) -- all with yaml struct tags
3. ProjectFilter struct with fields: Status (ProjectStatus), Tags ([]string), TechStack ([]string) -- all with yaml struct tags
4. AgentType (string type with constants: AgentClaudeCode='claude-code', AgentOpenClaw='openclaw')
5. AgentStatus (string type with constants: AgentIdle='idle', AgentBusy='busy', AgentOffline='offline')
6. Agent struct with fields: Name, Type (AgentType), Model, Capabilities ([]string), Role, Status (AgentStatus), LastSeen, HomeProject, ActiveTask, SessionCount (int), MemoryPath, WorkspacePath -- all with yaml struct tags
7. AgentFilter struct with fields: Type (AgentType), Capabilities ([]string), Status (AgentStatus) -- all with yaml struct tags
8. HiveKnowledgeResult struct with fields: ID, Project, ProjectPath, LocalID, Type, Topic, Summary, Detail, SourceTask, Date, Tags ([]string) -- all with yaml struct tags
9. HiveContext struct with fields: CurrentProject (Project), RelatedProjects ([]Project), RelatedKnowledge ([]HiveKnowledgeResult), ActiveAgents ([]Agent), CrossRepoPatterns ([]string), Summary (string) -- all with yaml struct tags

All yaml tags should be lowercase with underscores (e.g., `yaml:"repo_path"`). Use omitempty for optional slice fields.
acceptance_criteria: All 9 types defined with yaml struct tags

tasks.title: Create ProjectRegistry interface and implementation
tasks.description:
Create the internal/hive/ package with a ProjectRegistry. This is a Go project (module: github.com/valter-silva-au/ai-dev-brain). Create two files:

1. internal/hive/doc.go -- Package documentation: 'Package hive implements the Hive Mind layer for cross-project knowledge sharing and agent coordination.'

2. internal/hive/projectregistry.go -- Contains:
   - Import 'github.com/valter-silva-au/ai-dev-brain/pkg/models' for the Project and ProjectFilter types
   - ProjectRegistry interface with methods:
     * Register(project models.Project) error
     * Get(repoPathOrName string) (*models.Project, error)
     * List(filter models.ProjectFilter) ([]models.Project, error)
     * Load() error
     * Save() error
   - projectRegistryStore struct with fields: basePath (string), projects ([]models.Project)
   - Constructor: NewProjectRegistry(basePath string) ProjectRegistry
   - Implementation of all interface methods
   - Storage at basePath + '/projects/index.yaml' using gopkg.in/yaml.v3
   - The YAML file format is: version: '1.0', projects: [...]
   - Load() reads the file, Save() writes it atomically (write to .tmp, rename)
   - Register() adds or updates (match by RepoPath), sets LastUpdated to time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   - Get() matches by Name OR RepoPath
   - List() filters by Status, Tags (any match), TechStack (any match) -- empty filter returns all
   - File permissions: directories 0o755, files 0o644
   - Create directories with os.MkdirAll if they don't exist

tasks.title: Create ProjectRegistry unit tests
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/projectregistry_test.go with unit tests for the ProjectRegistry. Use standard Go testing with table-driven subtests. Every test must use t.TempDir() for filesystem isolation.

Test cases:
1. TestProjectRegistry_RegisterAndGet -- register a project, get by name, get by repo path, verify all fields
2. TestProjectRegistry_RegisterUpdate -- register same project twice (same RepoPath), verify it updates rather than duplicates
3. TestProjectRegistry_ListAll -- register 3 projects, list with empty filter, verify all 3 returned
4. TestProjectRegistry_ListFilterByStatus -- register 3 projects with different statuses, filter by 'active', verify only active returned
5. TestProjectRegistry_ListFilterByTags -- register projects with different tags, filter by tag, verify matches
6. TestProjectRegistry_ListFilterByTechStack -- register projects with different tech stacks, filter by 'go', verify matches
7. TestProjectRegistry_SaveAndLoad -- register projects, save, create new registry at same path, load, verify projects match
8. TestProjectRegistry_GetNotFound -- get a nonexistent project, verify nil returned with no error (or appropriate error)
9. TestProjectRegistry_EmptyRegistry -- list on empty registry returns empty slice, not nil

Use models.Project from github.com/valter-silva-au/ai-dev-brain/pkg/models.

tasks.title: Create KnowledgeAggregator interface and implementation
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/knowledgeaggregator.go in the existing internal/hive/ package. This aggregates knowledge across multiple project directories.

Create:
- SearchOptions struct with fields: ProjectFilter ([]string), TypeFilter ([]string), Since (time.Time), Limit (int) -- all with yaml struct tags
- KnowledgeAggregator interface with methods:
  * SearchAcrossProjects(query string, opts SearchOptions) ([]models.HiveKnowledgeResult, error)
  * GetDecisionsForTopic(topic string) ([]models.HiveKnowledgeResult, error)
  * Index() error
  * Load() error
  * Save() error
- knowledgeAggregatorStore struct with fields: basePath (string), projectReg (ProjectRegistry), entries ([]models.HiveKnowledgeResult), indexedAt (time.Time)
- Constructor: NewKnowledgeAggregator(basePath string, projectReg ProjectRegistry) KnowledgeAggregator

Implementation details:
- Storage at basePath + '/hive-mind/knowledge/index.yaml'
- YAML format: version: '1.0', indexed_at: timestamp, entries: [...]
- Index() walks all projects from ProjectRegistry, for each project reads '{project.RepoPath}/docs/knowledge/index.yaml' if it exists. Each knowledge entry has fields: id, type, topic, summary, detail, source_task, source_type, date. Namespace IDs as '{projectName}::{localID}'. Store results in entries slice.
- SearchAcrossProjects() does case-insensitive substring match on Summary and Topic fields. Respects ProjectFilter, TypeFilter, and Limit from SearchOptions.
- GetDecisionsForTopic() filters entries where Type=='decision' and Topic contains the query (case-insensitive)
- Load()/Save() read/write the index.yaml file using yaml.v3
- Use os.MkdirAll for directories, atomic writes (tmp + rename)
- File permissions: dirs 0o755, files 0o644

Import the existing knowledge YAML format. Read internal/core/knowledge.go to see the ExtractedKnowledge type for reference -- but do NOT import from core. Use the HiveKnowledgeResult model from pkg/models/hive.go.

For parsing project knowledge files, define a local struct to unmarshal the YAML:
```go
type knowledgeIndex struct {
    Version string `yaml:"version"`
    Entries []struct {
        ID         string `yaml:"id"`
        Type       string `yaml:"type"`
        Topic      string `yaml:"topic"`
        Summary    string `yaml:"summary"`
        Detail     string `yaml:"detail"`
        SourceTask string `yaml:"source_task"`
        SourceType string `yaml:"source_type"`
        Date       string `yaml:"date"`
    } `yaml:"entries"`
}
```

tasks.title: Create KnowledgeAggregator unit tests
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/knowledgeaggregator_test.go with unit tests. Use standard Go testing with table-driven subtests. Every test uses t.TempDir() for filesystem isolation.

For tests that need project knowledge, create the directory structure in the temp dir:
- Create a fake project dir with docs/knowledge/index.yaml containing test knowledge entries
- Register the project via ProjectRegistry before testing aggregation

Test cases:
1. TestKnowledgeAggregator_IndexEmptyRegistry -- index with no projects, verify no entries
2. TestKnowledgeAggregator_IndexSingleProject -- create a project dir with docs/knowledge/index.yaml containing 3 entries, register it, index, verify 3 entries in aggregator with namespaced IDs
3. TestKnowledgeAggregator_IndexMultipleProjects -- create 2 project dirs with knowledge, register both, index, verify entries from both projects
4. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SearchByQuery -- index projects, search for a term in summary, verify matching results
5. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SearchCaseInsensitive -- search with different case, verify matches
6. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SearchFilterByProject -- search with ProjectFilter set, verify only matching project's results
7. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SearchFilterByType -- search with TypeFilter set to 'decision', verify only decisions returned
8. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SearchWithLimit -- search with Limit=2, verify at most 2 results
9. TestKnowledgeAggregator_GetDecisionsForTopic -- add decisions with topics, query by topic, verify correct results
10. TestKnowledgeAggregator_SaveAndLoad -- index, save, create new aggregator, load, verify entries match
11. TestKnowledgeAggregator_ProjectWithNoKnowledge -- project exists but has no docs/knowledge/index.yaml, index should skip gracefully

The YAML format for test knowledge files:
```yaml
version: "1.0"
entries:
  - id: K-00001
    type: decision
    topic: architecture
    summary: Use local interfaces to avoid import cycles
    detail: detailed explanation
    source_task: TASK-00005
    source_type: task_archive
    date: "2026-02-01"
```

tasks.title: Create AgentRegistry interface and implementation
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/agentregistry.go in the existing internal/hive/ package.

Create:
- AgentRegistry interface with methods:
  * Register(agent models.Agent) error
  * Get(name string) (*models.Agent, error)
  * List(filter models.AgentFilter) ([]models.Agent, error)
  * DiscoverOpenClaw(openclawPath string) ([]models.Agent, error)
  * Load() error
  * Save() error
- agentRegistryStore struct with fields: basePath (string), agents ([]models.Agent)
- Constructor: NewAgentRegistry(basePath string) AgentRegistry

Implementation:
- Storage at basePath + '/agents/index.yaml'
- YAML format: version: '1.0', agents: [...]
- Register() adds or updates (match by Name), sets LastSeen to time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
- Get() matches by Name (case-insensitive)
- List() filters by Type, Capabilities (any match), Status -- empty filter returns all
- DiscoverOpenClaw(openclawPath) reads openclawPath + '/openclaw.json' to discover agents:
  * Parse the JSON: agents.list is an array of objects with 'id', 'name', 'workspace', 'agentDir' fields
  * For each agent entry, try to read agentDir + '/SOUL.md' for capabilities (extract first line after '## Objective' or '## Core Behaviors')
  * Create models.Agent with Type=models.AgentOpenClaw, Model read from config defaults, Status=models.AgentIdle
  * Return discovered agents (do NOT auto-register them)
- Load()/Save() read/write index.yaml
- Atomic writes, os.MkdirAll, permissions 0o755/0o644

For JSON parsing of openclaw.json, define local structs:
```go
type openclawConfig struct {
    Agents struct {
        Defaults struct {
            Model struct {
                Primary string `json:"primary"`
            } `json:"model"`
        } `json:"defaults"`
        List []struct {
            ID        string `json:"id"`
            Name      string `json:"name"`
            Workspace string `json:"workspace"`
            AgentDir  string `json:"agentDir"`
        } `json:"list"`
    } `json:"agents"`
}
```

tasks.title: Create AgentRegistry unit tests
Create MessageBus interface and implementation
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/messagebus.go in the existing internal/hive/ package. This is a file-based pub/sub messaging system.

First, add to pkg/models/hive.go (the file created in HIVE-001) a new type HiveMessageType and HiveMessageStatus:
```go
type HiveMessageType string
const (
    HiveMessageRequest  HiveMessageType = "request"
    HiveMessageResponse HiveMessageType = "response"
    HiveMessageNotify   HiveMessageType = "notify"
    HiveMessageQuery    HiveMessageType = "query"
)

type HiveMessageStatus string
const (
    HiveMessagePending   HiveMessageStatus = "pending"
    HiveMessageDelivered HiveMessageStatus = "delivered"
    HiveMessageRead      HiveMessageStatus = "read"
    HiveMessageArchived  HiveMessageStatus = "archived"
)

type HiveMessage struct {
    ID             string            `yaml:"id"`
    ConversationID string            `yaml:"conversation_id,omitempty"`
    From           string            `yaml:"from"`
    To             string            `yaml:"to"`
    Subject        string            `yaml:"subject"`
    Content        string            `yaml:"content"`
    Type           HiveMessageType   `yaml:"type"`
    Priority       string            `yaml:"priority"`
    Date           string            `yaml:"date"`
    InReplyTo      string            `yaml:"in_reply_to,omitempty"`
    Tags           []string          `yaml:"tags,omitempty"`
    Metadata       map[string]string `yaml:"metadata,omitempty"`
    Status         HiveMessageStatus `yaml:"status"`
}
```

Then create internal/hive/messagebus.go:
- MessageBus interface with methods:
  * Publish(msg models.HiveMessage) error
  * Subscribe(recipient string) ([]models.HiveMessage, error)
  * MarkProcessed(messageID string) error
  * GetConversation(conversationID string) ([]models.HiveMessage, error)
- messageBusStore struct with fields: basePath (string), counter (int)
- Constructor: NewMessageBus(basePath string) MessageBus

Implementation:
- Messages stored as individual YAML files
- Inbox: basePath + '/channels/inbox/{recipient}/{message-id}.yaml'
- Archive: basePath + '/channels/archive/{year}/{month}/{message-id}.yaml'
- Publish(): auto-generate ID as 'MSG-{counter:05d}', set Date to now UTC, Status to 'pending', write to inbox/{msg.To}/{id}.yaml
- Subscribe(): read all .yaml files from inbox/{recipient}/, parse, sort by Date, return
- MarkProcessed(): find message in any inbox, move to archive/{year}/{month}/
- GetConversation(): scan inbox and archive for messages with matching ConversationID
- Counter stored in basePath + '/.hive_counter' (same pattern as .task_counter: read int, increment, write)
- Atomic writes, os.MkdirAll, permissions 0o755/0o644
acceptance_criteria: pkg/models/hive.go updated with HiveMessage, HiveMessageType, HiveMessageStatus

tasks.title: Create MessageBus unit tests
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/messagebus_test.go with unit tests. Use standard Go testing with table-driven subtests. Every test uses t.TempDir().

Test cases:
1. TestMessageBus_PublishAndSubscribe -- publish a message, subscribe for recipient, verify message received with auto-generated ID and date
2. TestMessageBus_PublishMultiple -- publish 3 messages to same recipient, subscribe, verify all 3 returned sorted by date
3. TestMessageBus_SubscribeEmpty -- subscribe for nonexistent recipient, verify empty slice returned (not nil)
4. TestMessageBus_SubscribeDifferentRecipients -- publish to agent-a and agent-b, subscribe for agent-a, verify only agent-a's messages
5. TestMessageBus_MarkProcessed -- publish message, mark processed, subscribe again, verify message no longer in inbox
6. TestMessageBus_MarkProcessedCreatesArchive -- mark processed, verify file exists in archive/{year}/{month}/ directory
7. TestMessageBus_GetConversation -- publish 3 messages with same ConversationID, get conversation, verify all 3 returned
8. TestMessageBus_GetConversationMixed -- publish messages with different ConversationIDs, get one conversation, verify only matching returned
9. TestMessageBus_AutoIncrementID -- publish 3 messages, verify IDs are MSG-00001, MSG-00002, MSG-00003
10. TestMessageBus_CounterPersistence -- publish message, create new MessageBus at same path, publish another, verify ID continues from previous counter

For creating test messages, use:
```go
msg := models.HiveMessage{
    From:    "team-lead",
    To:      "researcher",
    Subject: "Research React hooks",
    Content: "Please research React hooks best practices",
    Type:    models.HiveMessageRequest,
    Priority: "P1",
}
```

tasks.title: Create Hive Mind integration test
tasks.description:
Create internal/hive/integration_test.go with integration tests that exercise the full Hive Mind workflow: project registration, knowledge indexing, agent discovery, and messaging.

Test cases:
1. TestIntegration_FullWorkflow:
   - Create temp dir as basePath
   - Create 2 fake project directories under basePath with:
     * docs/knowledge/index.yaml containing 2-3 knowledge entries each
   - Create a fake openclaw directory with openclaw.json containing 2 agents
   - Create ProjectRegistry, register both projects
   - Create KnowledgeAggregator, index
   - Verify: search returns entries from both projects
   - Create AgentRegistry, discover from fake openclaw dir
   - Register discovered agents
   - Verify: list agents returns both
   - Create MessageBus, publish message from one agent to another
   - Subscribe for recipient, verify message received
   - Mark processed, verify inbox empty

2. TestIntegration_SaveLoadRoundTrip:
   - Register projects, agents, index knowledge
   - Save all registries
   - Create fresh instances at same path
   - Load all
   - Verify all data survived the round trip

3. TestIntegration_EmptyEcosystem:
   - Create all components with empty basePath
   - Verify all operations return empty results, not errors
   - Verify no panics on empty state

Use t.TempDir() for all tests. Import from internal/hive and pkg/models only.
acceptance_criteria: Tests exercise ProjectRegistry + KnowledgeAggregator + AgentRegistry + MessageBus together
